prs.title: improved ui for 404 not found page
prs.body:
This pull request refactors and redesigns the `NotFound.jsx` page, focusing on a cleaner layout, improved visuals, and a simplified user experience. The changes remove the previous animated satellite, quick links, and fun fact sections, replacing them with a prominent 404 display, globe image, and a single "Go Home" button.

**UI Redesign and Simplification:**

* Replaced the previous animated astronaut/satellite and quick links grid with a minimal layout featuring a large "404" display, a globe image, and a clear "Go Home" button for navigation.
* Removed the fun fact and developer error code sections to streamline the page and reduce distractions.

**Visual Improvements:**

* Increased the number of background stars from 100 to 150 for a richer visual effect.
* Added a globe image with a glow effect to enhance the space theme and visual appeal.

**Code Cleanup and Modernization:**

* Refactored the component to use a named export (`export default function NotFound`) and simplified imports by removing unused icons and cleaning up the code.
* Updated layout classes for better responsiveness and alignment, ensuring the page looks good across devices.

prs.title: Improved ui for 404 not found page.
prs.body:
This pull request refactors and simplifies the `NotFound.jsx` page, focusing on improving the user experience and visual design. The main changes include removing unused code and quick links, updating the layout and background, and streamlining the main content to be more focused and visually appealing.

**UI and Layout Improvements:**

* The layout is redesigned to center the content and use a simple black background, improving readability and visual focus. The animated stars background is enhanced by increasing the number of stars from 100 to 150 for a richer effect.
* The main content now features a large "404" flanking a globe image, with a glow effect for visual emphasis. The previous astronaut/satellite animation and quick links grid have been removed for a cleaner design.

**Content and Navigation Changes:**

* The page text is simplified to a clear "Oops! Lost in Space" heading and a concise explanation, replacing the previous "Houston, we have a problem!" and fun fact section.
* Navigation is streamlined with a single "Go Home" button featuring an arrow icon, replacing the previous "Return to Home" and "Go Back" options. 

**Codebase Simplification:**

* Unused imports and components (such as icons and fun fact sections) are removed, and the file now uses a default export for the main function component.

prs.title: improved astronomy news ui
prs.body:
This pull request updates the UI of the `AstronomyNews` component to provide a more modern and visually appealing experience. The main changes include a redesigned header section, improved news card layouts with enhanced interactivity, and updated positioning for the scroll-to-top button.

**UI Redesigns and Enhancements:**

* Header section now features a badge, larger title, and a visually styled refresh button with improved positioning and accessibility.
* News grid layout is now responsive, using multiple columns and larger gaps for better readability on various screen sizes. News cards feature a gradient background, image hover effects, and a "Read more" button with an icon for improved user interaction.

**Minor UI Adjustments:**

* Scroll-to-top button position has been updated for consistency and better visibility.

prs.title: improved astronomy news ui
prs.body:
This pull request updates the `AstronomyNews` component to improve its visual design and user experience. The main changes focus on enhancing the header section, redesigning the news grid and cards for better readability and interactivity, and refining UI elements such as the "Scroll to Top" button.

**UI/UX Improvements**

* Redesigned the header section to include a badge ("Latest Updates"), a larger and more prominent title, and a visually enhanced refresh button with improved positioning and styling.

* Updated the news grid layout to use responsive CSS grid classes, improving how news articles are displayed across different screen sizes.

**News Card Enhancements**

* Enhanced each news card with a modern look: added image overlays, hover effects, and improved typography for titles and summaries. The "Read more" link is now a styled button with an icon, making it more interactive and accessible.

**Button Styling Adjustments**

* Refined the "Scroll to Top" button’s positioning and appearance for better visibility and consistency with the overall design.

prs.title: fixed responsiveness issue in events and improved  ui
prs.body:
This pull request updates the `Events` component to improve event filtering logic, enhance the event card UI/UX, and fix video playback behavior across devices. The most important changes are grouped below:

**Event Filtering Logic Improvements:**

* The `getFilteredEvents` function now combines data from `calendarEventsData.events` and `events` to ensure only events with images are shown, and filters based on the new `startDate` property instead of `date`. This makes filtering more accurate and consistent.

**Event Card UI/UX Enhancements:**

* Event cards are now rendered using `motion.div` from Framer Motion for smooth entry animations, and the card layout, styling, and responsiveness have been improved for a more polished look.
* A new date badge is displayed on each card, showing the event date in a user-friendly format, and an overlay gradient has been added for better text readability.
* The card content layout has been refined, with improved typography and spacing for event titles and descriptions.

**Video Playback and Interaction Fixes:**

* The hover/click logic for video playback now handles mobile and desktop separately, adds error handling for failed video playback, and updates selector references from `.card` to `.group` for consistency with new markup.
* Video and image elements have updated classes for improved transitions and layering, ensuring the video appears smoothly on hover and is non-interactive when not hovered.

**Minor Codebase Cleanups:**

* Removed unnecessary import of `index.css` from the component, as it is no longer needed.
* Adjusted padding and gap values in the filter navbar and event grid for better layout consistency across device sizes.

prs.title: improved navbar menu
prs.body:
This pull request refactors the `Navbar` component to enhance its design and user experience, especially for both desktop and mobile views. It introduces animated transitions, improves responsiveness, and modernizes the navigation with dropdown menus and motion effects. Additionally, a new contributor is added to the project.

**Navbar component redesign and UX improvements:**

* Refactored `Navbar.jsx` to use `framer-motion` for animated transitions, including showing/hiding the navbar based on scroll direction and adding smooth dropdowns and menu animations.
* Redesigned both desktop and mobile navbars with improved accessibility, visual styles, and responsive layouts. Dropdown menus for "Explore" and "Community" are now animated, and navigation items use a utility function for consistent styling.
* Removed legacy scroll and resize handlers in favor of motion-based visibility logic, simplifying the component and improving performance.

**Contributor update:**

* Added "Kapish rohilla" as a new contributor in `contributors.json` with profile details and repository association.

prs.title: added my story
prs.body:
# ✨ Open Source Learning Stories – Pull Request Template

Thank you for contributing to **Open Source Learning Stories**! 🎉
Please ensure your PR follows the guidelines below to help us review it quickly.

---

## ✅ Author Checklist

* [ ] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) guide.
* [ ] My story is added inside the **`stories/`** folder.
* [ ] The file is named correctly: **`<your-github-username>.md`**. (We recommend using your GitHub username for file naming consistency).
* [ ] I used the proper story format (Name, Date, Tools, Experience, Advice).
* [ ] I did not edit or delete someone else’s story.
* [ ] My contribution is genuine and respectful.

---

## 📖 Description of Changes

Please provide a clear and brief description of your contribution.

(e.g., “Added my personal open-source journey story,” “Fixed a typo in the Java section of the README,” etc.)

---

## 🔗 Related Issue (Optional)

If this Pull Request resolves an existing issue, please link it here.

* Closes # (issue number)

---

## 🖼️ Screenshots / Preview (Optional)

If your contribution involves significant text changes or documentation fixes, you can provide a preview here.

---

🎉 **Tip:** Thank you again for being a part of this project! We appreciate your story!

prs.title: added answer to question 43 in javascript
prs.body:
This pull request adds a new JavaScript program to calculate the average of numbers in an array. The function handles empty arrays and demonstrates usage with an example.

* Added `calculateAverage` function to compute the average of array elements, including a safeguard for empty arrays. (`javascript/q43_calculate_average_using_arrays.js`)
* Included example usage and console output to demonstrate the function. (`javascript/q43_calculate_average_using_arrays.js`)

This pull request updates the `AstronomyNews` component with a major UI redesign to enhance visual appeal and user experience. The changes focus on improving the header section, refreshing the layout of news articles, and updating interactive elements for better usability and consistency.

### UI/UX Improvements

* Redesigned the header section to include a badge for "Latest Updates", a larger and more visually prominent title, and repositioned the refresh button for improved accessibility and appearance.
* Enhanced the news grid layout using responsive CSS grid classes, allowing for a flexible arrangement across different screen sizes.

### News Article Card Redesign

* Updated news article cards with a new visual style: added image overlays, improved hover effects, clearer typography for titles and summaries, and a "Read more" button with an animated arrow icon for better call-to-action.

### Interactive Element Updates

* Changed the "Scroll to Top" button’s positioning and styling for better visibility and consistency with the new design.

prs.title: enhanced events timeline ui with timeline animations
prs.body:
This pull request significantly enhances the `Events` page by introducing animated scroll-driven timelines and improving the overall layout and interactivity. The main focus is on making the timeline visually dynamic using Framer Motion, refactoring the layout for better responsiveness, and polishing event cards for a more modern look. Below are the most important changes:

**Timeline Animation & Interactivity**

* The timeline section now uses Framer Motion's `useScroll` and `useTransform` hooks to animate the timeline line and event appearance based on scroll position. This includes animated lines, glowing effects, and smooth fade/scale-in of timeline items. (`src/components/Events.jsx`)

**Layout & Responsiveness**

* The layout of the events page is refactored for improved responsiveness and clarity. The filter navbar and main content are now better organized for both desktop and mobile views. (`src/components/Events.jsx`)
* Added a top margin to the main content area for better spacing from the video background. (`src/index.css`)

**Event Card Visuals**

* Event cards now feature rounded corners, smoother hover/scale effects, and lazy loading for images. Video overlays are also styled for consistency. (`src/components/Events.jsx`)

**Code Structure & Cleanup**

* Legacy commented-out code and duplicate layout logic have been removed for clarity and maintainability. (`src/components/Events.jsx`)

**Minor Data Fix**

* Minor formatting fix in `contributors.json` for consistency. (`src/data/contributors.json`)

prs.title: Batman Animation
prs.body:
Thank you for making a pull request to ZTM's Animation Nation project. We will review your pull request as soon as we can, but keep in mind we can get very busy during Hacktoberfest.

While you're waiting, please look over this checklist. Feel free to write a description of what you did as well!

## What I did

*Optional: Edit this to tell us what you did!*

## Pull request checklist

- [ ] This pull request uses **HTML and CSS** only and provides a `meta.json` file.
- [ ] All files in this pull request are in a directory named: `<github_username>-<art_name>` without the <>.
- [ ] Your pull request contains `index.html` and `styles.css`.
	- [ ] Your html file is named exactly: `index.html`
	- [ ] Your css file is named exactly: `styles.css`
- [ ] Your pull request your `meta.json` with:
	- [ ] `githubHandle`: being your unique github user name,
	- [ ] `artName` : name of your animation
- [ ] Your submission includes **at least one animation**.
- [ ] Your pull request **does not modify any other files** in the repository.

#### Not normally needed
- [ ] Check this if you would like to discuss an improvement besides adding animations

**Note: if your pull request makes an improvement besides adding a CSS animation you will need to discuss this with the maintainers. You can help kickstart this process by explaining what your change does and why you are making it in the "What I did" section**

## What to expect

- If your pull request meets the checklist requirements it will be approved and merged by a maintainer
- If your pull request needs some changes. A maintainer will leave a comment requesting changes
- If your pull request significantly violates the criteria in the checklist it will be closed with feedback.
